title: Medical Doctor (MBChB)
description: Diagnose and treat illness, injury, and disease. Doctors work in hospitals, clinics, and private practice across every medical speciality — from general practice to surgery.

employability.demand: Very high — acute shortage of doctors in public and rural health; on Critical Skills List.
employability.outlook: Exceptional; healthcare demand grows with population and disease burden.
pathways: Internship, specialisation (3–6 years), private or public practice

title: Pharmacist
description: Dispense medicines, counsel patients, and ensure safe and effective drug use. Pharmacists work in retail pharmacies, hospitals, the pharmaceutical industry, and regulatory bodies.

employability.demand: High — shortage in hospital pharmacy and rural areas.
employability.outlook: Stable; pharmaceutical sector growth adds industrial and research roles.
pathways: Retail pharmacy chains, hospitals, pharmaceutical companies

title: Physiotherapist
description: Restore and improve patients' movement and function through exercise, manual therapy, and rehabilitation. Work with sports injuries, post-surgical recovery, neurological conditions, and chronic pain.

employability.demand: High — shortage particularly in public hospitals and rural areas.
employability.outlook: Growing; sports science and private rehab clinics expanding.
pathways: Hospitals, sports teams, private practices, rehabilitation centres

title: Occupational Therapist
description: Help people with physical, mental, or developmental challenges perform daily activities and meaningful work. Work in hospitals, schools, rehabilitation centres, and private practice.

employability.demand: High — shortage in public health facilities; schools need OTs for learner support.
employability.outlook: Growing; ageing population and disability rights increasing demand.

title: Registered Nurse
description: Provide direct patient care — monitoring health, administering treatment, supporting doctors, and educating patients. Nurses are the backbone of healthcare delivery in hospitals and clinics.

employability.demand: Very high — acute nursing shortage in SA; on Critical Skills List.
employability.outlook: Exceptional; public healthcare system is severely understaffed.
pathways: 4-year diploma or degree at nursing college or university
employability.employment_note: Primary pathway is a 4-year nursing college diploma. University degrees (BSc Nursing) exist at some institutions but most nurses train at SANC-accredited colleges.

title: Dietitian / Nutritionist
description: Assess and treat nutritional conditions — from managing diabetes and eating disorders to optimising athlete performance. Provide evidence-based dietary advice in clinical and community settings.

employability.demand: Moderate — growing awareness of nutrition driving demand in private practice.
employability.outlook: Stable; wellness, sports, and chronic disease management creating new opportunities.
pathways: Hospitals, sports teams, private practice, food industry

title: Dentist
description: Diagnose and treat conditions of the teeth and mouth — fillings, extractions, orthodontics, and oral surgery. Work in private practice, hospitals, or public dental clinics.

employability.demand: High — shortage of dentists in public sector and rural areas.
employability.outlook: Strong; private practice demand is consistent.
pathways: Private practices, public dental clinics, hospitals

title: Radiographer / Radiologist
description: Use imaging technology — X-rays, MRI, CT scans, and ultrasound — to diagnose disease and injury. Diagnostic radiographers capture the images; radiologists (doctors) interpret them.

employability.demand: High — shortage of radiographers across public and private hospitals.
employability.outlook: Growing; medical imaging is expanding in diagnostics and oncology.

title: Speech-Language Therapist
description: Assess and treat communication and swallowing disorders in children and adults — from stuttering and language delay to post-stroke speech recovery.

employability.demand: High — critical shortage, especially in schools and public hospitals.
employability.outlook: Growing; increased awareness of learning disabilities driving school demand.

title: Biomedical Scientist / Medical Technologist
description: Perform laboratory tests on blood, tissue, and other samples to diagnose disease. Work behind the scenes in hospital labs and private pathology laboratories.

employability.demand: Moderate-high — laboratory services are essential to healthcare delivery.
employability.outlook: Stable; pandemic heightened awareness of diagnostic lab capacity needs.
pathways: BSc Medical Laboratory Science / Biomedical Science (4 years)
